title: -Landmarks Preservation Commission Appeal: 2138 Kittredge, Landmark application #LMIN2024-0006

sections.recommendation:
Conduct a public hearing, and upon conclusion adopt a Resolution upholding the appeal
and reversing the Landmarks Preservation Commission (LPC) decision to designate the
subject property as a City Landmark.
SUMMARY
On February 6, 2025, the LPC approved Landmark Designation Application
#LMIN2024-0006, designating the property at 2138 Kittredge Street as a City
Landmark. The designation was based on findings that the property meets criteria
related to architectural merit, cultural value, and historical significance.
On March 24, 2025, StudioOne QOZB LLC, the applicant for a pending Use Permit on
the same property (#ZP2024-0114) which was submitted pursuant to Senate Bill 330
(the Housing Crisis Act of 2019), filed an appeal of the LPC’s decision. The appellant
contends that landmark designation of the subject property would conflict with the
provisions of SB 330 and adversely impact the proposed housing development project.
sections.current_situation:
On February 14, 2024 the City received a Preliminary Use Permit (PLN2024-0011)
application pursuant to SB330.
On April 4, 2024 the Preliminary Use Permit (PLN2024-0011) was deemed complete
and vested to that date.
On August 6, 2024, the appellant submitted a Use Permit application pursuant to SB
330.

LPC Appeal: 2138 Kittredge Street, Landmark Application LMIN2024-0006 PUBLIC HEARING
July 23, 2025
On September 5, 2024, the Use Permit application (#ZP2024-0114) was deemed
complete.
On October 30, 2024, the City received a Landmark or Structure of Merit application for
the subject property and initiated consideration of the property for the designation.
In December 2024, staff prepared notices for a public hearing scheduled for the LPC
meeting of January 6, 2025. However, due to concerns raised by interested parties, the
item was pulled from the agenda.
On January 27, 2025, the City mailed public hearing notices to property owners and
occupants within 300 feet of the project site, as well as to interested neighborhood
organizations.
On February 6, 2025, the LPC held a public hearing on the landmark designation
application and received public comment. Following deliberations, the LPC unanimously
approved the designation by a vote of 8-0-0-0 vote (Moved: Schwartz, Second: Orbuch,
Ayes: Crandall, Enchill, Finacom, Leuschner, Montgomery, Orbuch, Plese and
Schwartz, Noes: None; Abstain: None; Absent: None). See Attachment 3 for the LPC
staff report.
On March 24, 2025, StudioOne QOZB LLC filed an appeal of the LPC’s decision.
sections.background:
Landmarks Designation
The subject property is a 6,750-square-foot lot located on the south side of Kittredge
Street, between Shattuck Avenue and Fulton Street. It is located in the C-DMU
(Downtown Mixed-Use) Outer Core Zoning District (Figure 1) and contains two
structures: a two-story residence and a detached carport (Image 1 and Image 2).
Page 2
Page 3 of 137
LPC Appeal: 2138 Kittredge Street, Landmark Application LMIN2024-0006 PUBLIC HEARING
July 23, 2025
Figure 1. Zoning
The primary structure, historically known as the Fitzpatrick House and later as the
Pepper Tree Tea Room, is situated near the northwest corner of the lot, with its north-
facing façade built directly along the northern property line. The one-story carport is
located at the rear of the site, occupying the southwest corner and spanning the full
width of the rear property line. Vehicle access is provided by a gated driveway at the
northwest corner of the parcel, running along the eastern side of the residence.
Page 3

The subject property is not listed on the National Register of Historic Places (NR) or the
California Register of Historical Resources (CR), but it was recorded for the State
Historic Resources Inventory in 1978 and recommended eligible for the NR. It is also
listed on the Berkeley Urban Conservation Survey (form #21280).
In 2015, the City completed the Shattuck Avenue Commercial Corridor Historic Context
and Survey, which identified a potential Shattuck Avenue Commercial Corridor Historic
District. The subject property was assigned a California Historical Resource Status
Code (CHRSC) of 3S, signifying it as locally significant and a contributor to the
proposed district under Criterion A/1 of the NR/CR, w
sections.rationale:
The main issue raised in the appellant’s letter (Attachment 2) and staff’s response are
detailed below. For the sake of brevity, all the appeal issues are not re-stated in their
entirety.
Summary of Main Issue: The appellant claims that the designation of the property
as a historic site is prohibited by SB 330 and the HAA due to rights vested by the
complete housing development project application, and that it places an undue
burden, putting the project at risk.
Page 5
Page 6 of 137
LPC Appeal: 2138 Kittredge Street, Landmark Application LMIN2024-0006 PUBLIC HEARING
July 23, 2025
Response: Under State law, once a preliminary application for a housing development
project is submitted and deemed complete, it is protected from the application of new
policies or standards, including historic designations. Government Code Section
65913.10(a) (SB 330) requires that a city determine whether a project site is a historic
site at the time the application is deemed complete, and that determination remains
valid for the duration of the project review. Similarly, Government Code Section
65589.5(o)(1) (HAA) provides that housing projects are subject only to the ordinances,
policies, and standards that were in effect at the time a preliminary application was
submitted.
In this case, the preliminary application was deemed completed on April 4, 2024 and the
full application was deemed complete on September 5, 2024. The Landmarks
Preservation Commission designated the site as a historic landmark on February 6,
2025, which occurred after the vested rights had been established. Accordingly, Staff
recommends reversing the designation because it cannot be applied to the proposed
project under State law. As a result, the designation would have no practical effect in
this instance.
sections.alternatives:
Pursuant to BMC Section 3.24.300(E), the Council may (1) continue the public hearing,
(2) affirm, or modify the LPC’s decision, or (3) remand the matter to the LPC.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The reversal of 2138 Kittredge Street as a City Landmark designation, LMIN2024-0006,
would not have a significant effect on the environment, and therefore is not subject to
CEQA (CEQA Guidelines Section 15061(b)(3), Common Sense Exemption).
CONTACT PERSON
Jordan Klein, Director, Planning & Development Department, (510) 981-7534
